message:
fix(parsing): harden Digest auth and multipart Content-Disposition parsing

digest.rs — Unterminated quoted values in Digest auth headers were
silently accepted because the KV parser loop could exit without
detecting a missing closing quote. Add a `closed` flag that tracks
whether the terminating `"` was found, and return an error if the
value ends without one. This prevents malformed Authorization headers
from being interpreted as valid credentials with truncated values.

multipart.rs — Content-Disposition parameter names (name=, filename=)
were matched via hardcoded prefix stripping with only two case
variants ("name=" and "NAME="). RFC 2183 requires case-insensitive
matching. Refactor to split on '=' first and use
eq_ignore_ascii_case() for the key comparison, handling all case
combinations (e.g. "Name=", "FILENAME=", "fileName=").

Add tests:
- parse_rejects_unterminated_quoted_value: verifies malformed Digest
  header with missing closing quote is rejected
- test_parse_content_disposition_case_insensitive_params: verifies
  mixed-case Name/FileName parameters are parsed correctly
- test_parse_simple_form_with_mixed_case_disposition_params: full
  multipart parse with mixed-case params

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
